steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The claim is that the narrator has a 'strong emotional attachment to the surrounding forests.' We need a quotation where she directly expresses personal emotional feelings toward the woods themselves.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A: the narrator describes parental neglect of the landscape—no personal emotional feeling toward forests. ✗
  • Choice B: 'My favourites' and 'it makes my heart bleed to see one of those cut down'—both phrases directly express personal affection and emotional pain toward specific trees. ✓
  • Choice C: describes the landscape's appearance, not the narrator's feelings about it. ✗
  • Choice D: mentions activities that occur in forests but no emotional attachment to them. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
